title: GHK-CU
description: GHK-Cu (Copper peptide) is a naturally occurring tripeptide with copper bound to it. It promotes wound healing, collagen synthesis, and has anti-aging properties. Used topically for skin rejuvenation and systemically for tissue repair.

research.title: In vivo stimulation of connective tissue accumulation by the tripeptide-copper complex glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine-Cu2+
research.summary: GHK-Cu increases extracellular matrix accumulation in wounds in vivo, potentially promoting wound repair and healing.

research.title: The tripeptide-copper complex glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine-Cu2+ stimulates matrix metalloproteinase-2 expression by fibroblast cultures
research.summary: GHK-Cu stimulates MMP-2 expression in wound fibroblasts, promoting connective tissue production and remodeling.

research.title: Stimulation of sulfated glycosaminoglycan synthesis by the tripeptide-copper complex glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine-Cu2+
research.summary: GHK-Cu stimulates glycosaminoglycan synthesis in human fibroblasts, potentially contributing to its wound healing properties.

research.title: Glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine-Cu2+ attenuates cigarette smoke-induced pulmonary emphysema and inflammation by reducing oxidative stress pathway
research.summary: GHK-Cu treatment reduces cigarette smoke-induced emphysema and inflammation by reducing oxidative stress, offering potential for COPD.

research.title: The Human Tripeptide GHK-Cu in Prevention of Oxidative Stress and Degenerative Conditions of Aging
research.summary: GHK tripeptide shows potential as a therapeutic agent against age-associated neurodegeneration and cognitive decline.

research.title: Effects of GHK-Cu on MMP and TIMP Expression, Collagen and Elastin Production, and Facial Wrinkle Parameters
research.summary: GHK-Cu with nano-carriers significantly increases collagen and elastin production in human dermal fibroblasts, reducing facial wrinkle volume.

research.title: Self-Assembled Fluorescent and Antibacterial GHK-Cu Nanoparticles for Wound Healing Applications
research.summary: Self-assembled GHK-Cu nanoparticles show good biocompatibility and enhanced wound healing applications, offering antibacterial properties.

sentiment.summary: Users consistently report improvements in skin quality, wound healing, and hair growth. Both topical and injectable forms are popular. Considered one of the safer peptides.

effects.label: Visible Results:
effects.description: Skin improvements often noticeable within weeks.

effects.label: Well-Researched:
effects.description: Substantial body of research supporting benefits.

dosage.label: Injectable:
dosage.description: 1-2mg subcutaneously daily or several times per week.

sideEffects.label: Skin Irritation:
sideEffects.description: Rarely, topical application may cause mild irritation.

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Found in skincare products and from peptide suppliers.

availability.label: Multiple Forms:
availability.description: Available as serums, creams, and injectable powder.

availability.label: Reasonable Cost:
availability.description: Relatively affordable compared to many peptides.
